page: Amazon Web Services m8id.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. This x86_64 architecture server features 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The hardware configuration includes 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. The CPU features a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX).

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, achieving 7680.87 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 385.12 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Multi-threaded operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by a top-tier bzip3 decompression rate of 658.26 GB/sec and a strong multi-core stress-ng CPU score. However, single-core performance is relatively moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance placing in the middle 50% tier. For large language model inference, the hardware excels at prompt processing, scoring in the top 10% across small, medium, and large models, whereas text generation speeds drop to average or strong tiers.

The inclusion of high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage combined with high-speed DDR5 memory provides a resource-dense profile that is highly cost-efficient for data-intensive applications. It eliminates the need for external storage attachments for high-throughput workloads. This server is suited for database operations, as reflected in its top-tier Passmark database score and Redis SET performance of 169.45 million operations per second. Other appropriate workloads include static web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m8id.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 48 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores, and 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Utilizing the Nitro hypervisor on x86_64 architecture, it integrates 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The processor operates at 3.9 GHz with a 480 MB L3 cache and supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instructions. Benchmark data indicates top-tier memory read bandwidth and multi-threaded performance, though single-core CPU stress tests show average results. The instance is highly efficient for data-dense applications, showing top-tier capabilities in database operations, Redis SET performance, and software compilation. It is suited for high-throughput database hosting, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 5700 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and multi-threaded performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.24xlarge features 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 5.7 TB NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput database workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, rapid DDR5 memory, and extensive local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 384 GB of 7 GHz DDR5 memory on x86_64 architecture
5700 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
Top-tier memory read bandwidth and multi-threaded decompression
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated workloads
Suited for database operations, Redis, and software compilation
